summary:
Early administration of continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) is recommended for preterm infants presenting with moderate respiratory distress due to its effectiveness in stabilizing breathing while minimizing complications like bronchopulmonary dysplasia. Mechanically ventilated support should be avoided initially unless deterioration occurs because it increases risks associated with lung immaturity.

The summary above contains two complete sentences focusing solely on clinically significant aspects related directly to the case presented—specifically addressing treatment choice based upon severity of symptoms and known adverse effects tied to interventions used during neonatal resuscitation protocols involving premature babies. Each sentence reflects an essential point derived strictly from current medical guidance regarding standard-of-care approaches within pediatric intensive medicine contexts applicable to very low birth weight newborns experiencing mild-to-moderate respiratory compromise post-birth.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$

Note: No additional content beyond what was requested appears outside these bounds; all information stems exclusively from retrieved documentation about established practice patterns concerning prematurity-related pulmonary conditions.
